flashSynonyms.context: This is a ____ solution to the problem.

flashSynonyms.context: Pollution has ____ effects on the environment.

flashSynonyms.context: Students can ____ valuable skills through internships.
flashSynonyms.explanation: 'Acquire' (C1) es la opción más formal y precisa. 'Obtain' (B2) es buena alternativa. 'Get/have' son demasiado informales para PAU.

flashSynonyms.context: Many experts ____ that renewable energy is essential.
flashSynonyms.explanation: 'Maintain' (C1) es formal y académico. 'Consider' (B2) también funciona. Evita 'think/feel/believe' para elevar el nivel léxico.

flashSynonyms.explanation: 'Facilitate' (C1) demuestra vocabulario avanzado. 'Support' (B2) es alternativa válida. 'Help' es básico y debe evitarse.

flashSynonyms.context: Climate change poses a ____ challenge for humanity.
flashSynonyms.explanation: 'Substantial' (C1) es la opción más académica. 'Major' (B2) es aceptable. 'Big/large/huge' son demasiado coloquiales.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Topic: Should students wear school uniforms? Write a paragraph giving your opinion.

paragraphs.text: I think school uniforms are good because they are cheap and everyone looks the same. Many students like them and they are easy to wear. Also, you don't have to think about what to wear every day.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Usa 'I think' (evitar en PAU), estructuras simples, vocabulario básico ('good', 'cheap'). Sin conectores sofisticados ni voz pasiva.

paragraphs.text: School uniforms offer significant advantages. Firstly, they promote equality by eliminating visible socioeconomic differences. Furthermore, uniforms foster a sense of belonging and school identity. Although some argue they restrict self-expression, the benefits of reduced peer pressure outweigh this concern.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B2/C1. Excelente: usa conectores variados (Firstly, Furthermore, Although), vocabulario sofisticado ('promote equality', 'socioeconomic differences', 'foster'), estructura compleja con subordinadas.

paragraphs.text: In my opinion, school uniforms are beneficial because they help students focus on studies rather than fashion. Moreover, they reduce bullying related to clothing. However, some students feel uniforms limit their creativity and personal style.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Bueno: usa 'In my opinion' (mejor que 'I think'), conectores (Moreover, However), vocabulario intermedio. Falta voz pasiva y construcciones más complejas para nivel C1.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: En la PAU evita siempre 'I think'. Usa conectores de nivel B2+ (Furthermore, Nevertheless, Consequently). La voz pasiva demuestra madurez léxica.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Topic: The impact of social media on teenagers. Present both advantages and disadvantages.

paragraphs.text: Social media has good and bad things. It is good because you can talk to friends. But it is bad because people spend too much time on it. Many teenagers use Instagram and TikTok every day.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2. Estructuras muy básicas, repetición de 'it is good/bad', vocabulario elemental. Sin conectores formales ni introducción neutral necesaria en ensayos 'For and Against'.

paragraphs.text: Social media presents both advantages and drawbacks for teenagers. On the one hand, these platforms facilitate communication and enable access to educational content. On the other hand, excessive use can lead to anxiety and reduced face-to-face interaction. Ultimately, balanced usage is essential.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Perfecto para PAU: introducción neutral sin opinión personal, conectores formales (On the one hand, On the other hand, Ultimately), voz pasiva, vocabulario preciso ('facilitate', 'drawbacks', 'excessive use').

paragraphs.text: I believe social media has positive and negative effects. The advantages include staying connected with friends and learning new things. However, the disadvantages are cyberbullying and addiction. In conclusion, teenagers should use social media carefully.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Usa 'I believe' (evitar en introducción neutral). Tiene conectores (However, In conclusion) pero vocabulario podría ser más sofisticado ('carefully' → 'judiciously').

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Ensayos 'For and Against' requieren introducción NEUTRAL. Usa 'On the one hand/On the other hand'. Concluye con 'Ultimately/All things considered'.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about Remote work: advantages and disadvantages. (140-180 words)

paragraphs.text: I am agree that remote work is very good. It is a nice thing because you stay at home and things are easy. I like it very much. Working in the office is bad and very boring for me.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. El texto muestra una Gramática deficiente con interferencias claras como 'I am agree'. El Vocabulario es extremadamente pobre, basado en palabras baúl ('good', 'nice', 'things') y el Registro es demasiado informal para la PAU.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, professional flexibility is significantly enhanced when tasks are performed remotely. Notwithstanding the potential for social isolation, digital connectivity ensures that collaboration remains seamless. Consequently, overhead costs for companies are reduced while productivity is often maximized. It is widely maintained that this paradigm shift fosters a healthier work-life equilibrium, which is paramount in the modern corporate landscape.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Muestra una excelente Coherencia con conectores de nivel avanzado (Notwithstanding, Consequently). El uso de la voz pasiva eleva el Registro y el Vocabulario es académico y preciso ('paramount', 'equilibrium'), cumpliendo con la madurez discursiva exigida.

paragraphs.text: Remote work has many benefits for employees. Also, I think that people can save a lot of time because they do not have to travel to the office. However, it can be lonely sometimes. But, in my opinion, the flexibility is very good for families.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Aunque la Gramática es correcta, la Organización es básica debido al uso de conectores simples (Also, But). Para llegar al rango TOP, se debe evitar el abuso de 'I think' y mejorar la riqueza léxica sustituyendo adjetivos comunes.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Para lograr el 'Wow Effect' en Andalucía, sustituye conectores básicos por avanzados y usa la voz pasiva para dar objetividad a tus argumentos.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about Should university education be free? (130-150 words)

paragraphs.text: In my opinion university should be free. It is very good for kids and things are easy. People like it very much because study is nice. Pay for degree is bad.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. El Vocabulario es pobre y repetitivo, abusando de 'palabras baúl' (good, nice, things) penalizadas en la rúbrica de Valencia. La Gramática es demasiado simple y la Coherencia se ve afectada por la ausencia de conectores lógicos.

blocks.text: In today's globalized world, learning foreign languages has become increasingly important. Multilingualism offers numerous cognitive, professional, and cultural advantages that extend far beyond basic communication skills.

Firstly, acquiring a second language enhances cognitive abilities. Research demonstrates that bilingual individuals exhibit improved memory, problem-solving skills, and multitasking capabilities. Furthermore, language learning delays cognitive decline in elderly populations.

Secondly, professional opportunities expand significantly for multilingual candidates. In an interconnected global economy, employers increasingly value employees who can communicate across cultural boundaries. Moreover, language skills often lead to higher salaries and more diverse career paths.

In conclusion, the benefits of learning foreign languages are undeniable. From cognitive enhancement to professional advancement, multilingualism enriches both individual lives and society as a whole. Environmental protection requires collaborative efforts across all levels of society to ensure a sustainable future for coming generations.

textReconstructor.topic: The Impact of Social Media on Adolescence: A Balanced Analysis

blocks.text: The digital landscape has undergone a radical transformation, significantly altering adolescent interactions. It is frequently argued that social media platforms are indispensable for modern socialization. However, what remains a subject of intense debate is the profound impact these virtual environments exert on the psychological development of the younger generation.

Notwithstanding the risks, these platforms enable teenagers to foster meaningful global connections. Not only are geographical barriers dismantled by digital connectivity, but diverse communities are also formed where individual identities can flourish. Information is accessed instantaneously, providing educational resources that were previously inconceivable in traditional classrooms.

Conversely, substantial evidence suggests that excessive screen time is often linked to diminished self-esteem. Vulnerable adolescents are being constantly exposed to unrealistic standards of perfection, which may result in severe anxiety. Furthermore, unless privacy settings are strictly managed, personal data could be exploited by third parties without explicit user consent.

On balance, were users to exercise greater discernment, the benefits would certainly outweigh the pitfalls. Educational systems must prioritize media literacy to ensure digital well-being. Ultimately, the responsibility lies in achieving an equilibrium where technology serves human growth rather than dictating the terms of our social reality.

textReconstructor.topic: The Shift Toward Sustainability: Eco-Villages and Climate Change

blocks.text: Environmental degradation has reached a point where immediate global action is necessitated. It is widely acknowledged that industrial pollution and urban sprawl are the primary catalysts of this crisis. What is required is a paradigm shift in our living arrangements to effectively mitigate the irreversible damage inflicted upon the planet.

Establishing eco-villages represents a sophisticated approach to sustainable development. Within these communities, carbon footprints are significantly curtailed through organic farming and renewable energy integration. Notwithstanding the initial implementation costs, these self-sufficient settlements provide a clear blueprint for harmonious existence, reducing our current dependency on finite fossil fuels.

Never has the need for waste-to-energy systems been more paramount than in contemporary urban planning. In such sustainable environments, domestic refuse is converted into clean electricity, thus eliminating environmental toxicity. Should more districts adopt these principles, a substantial reduction in pollution would be achieved, fostering a resilient future for biodiversity.

If governments were to subsidize these sustainable initiatives, the transition toward ecological stability would accelerate. Eco-village principles are regarded as a call for collective responsibility. Ultimately, humanity must embrace these innovative strategies, provided that we wish to safeguard our natural heritage for the generations yet to come.

textReconstructor.topic: Academic Accessibility: Should University Education be Tuition-Free?

blocks.text: Whether higher education should be accessible to all regardless of financial status is a pivotal debate in modern society. It is widely maintained that talent remains untapped when tuition fees are excessive. Notwithstanding the economic burden on the state, providing free university education is regarded as an investment in human capital.

Eliminating financial barriers ensures that meritocracy prevails over privilege. Only if academic pathways are fully subsidized can social mobility be genuinely achieved for the most disadvantaged citizens. Furthermore, the intellectual growth of a nation is significantly enhanced when diverse perspectives are permitted to flourish within academic institutions, free from debt.

Moreover, a highly educated workforce is necessitated by the rapid advancements in the global technology sector. By removing tuition costs, governments would guarantee a steady supply of skilled professionals capable of navigating complex industrial challenges. It is the provision of free education that often serves as the cornerstone for long-term national prosperity.

Were governments to prioritize education over military spending, the societal benefits would be immeasurable. Ensuring universal access to universities is paramount for fostering an equitable future. Ultimately, the focus must shift towards empowering every individual to reach their potential, thereby strengthening the collective intelligence and moral fabric of our entire civilization.

textReconstructor.topic: The Dual Nature of Artificial Intelligence in Pedagogical Frameworks

blocks.text: It is the rapid integration of artificial intelligence that has revolutionized contemporary pedagogical frameworks. While some educators embrace this shift, others remain skeptical about the erosion of human intuition. Notwithstanding the controversy, the implementation of automated tools is increasingly considered an irreversible milestone in global educational evolution.

Furthermore, tailored learning experiences are facilitated by sophisticated algorithms that adapt to individual student requirements. Never has the potential for customized curricula been so attainable within traditional school settings. Consequently, learners are empowered to master complex concepts at their own pace, significantly reducing achievement gaps often observed in overcrowded classrooms.

Conversely, the integrity of academic evaluation could be undermined by the misuse of generative platforms. Critics argue that critical thinking skills might be neglected if answers are consistently provided by machines. Should pedagogical experts fail to address these ethical dilemmas, the very essence of genuine intellectual growth would likely perish.

On balance, if educational institutions were to implement strict ethical guidelines, technology would act as a catalyst for excellence. A balanced approach must be adopted to ensure that human guidance remains paramount. Ultimately, the focus should reside on fostering a synergy where digital tools enhance rather than replace the teacher.

textReconstructor.topic: Formal Letter: Escalating Noise Pollution in the City Center

blocks.text: It is with profound frustration that I am writing to express my indignation regarding the escalating noise levels in our neighbourhood. For some time, tranquility has been compromised by nocturnal disturbances. It is this persistent negligence that has been brought to your attention previously to no avail.

Seldom has our community encountered such flagrant violations of bylaws. Recently, heavy machinery was operated well beyond permitted hours, causing significant distress. Notwithstanding legal restrictions, disruptive activities are being performed with impunity, while citizen reports are being systematically overlooked by the local council.

Moreover, the situation is exacerbated by the unregulated influx of late-night revelry. Substantial evidence suggests that enforcement is inadequate. Were rigorous inspections to be conducted, our quality of life would be enhanced. It is paramount that the fundamental right to rest is prioritized over commercial gains.

All things considered, I request that immediate measures be implemented. Should the council fail to address this predicament, legal action will be sought. If a comprehensive noise-monitoring scheme were established, we would finally enjoy the residential peace that is routinely denied to us.

textReconstructor.topic: The Socio-Economic Impact of Tourism: A Regional Assessment

blocks.text: The economic landscape of our coastal municipality has been profoundly reshaped by the seasonal influx of international visitors. It is widely acknowledged that local businesses depend heavily on this revenue. However, what necessitates urgent analysis is the increasing strain placed upon our natural resources and urban infrastructure due to unregulated expansion.

Substantial financial growth is generated through the hospitality sector, providing livelihoods for numerous families. Not only has the town's international profile been enhanced, but significant investments have also been made in public transport and cultural heritage restoration. Consequently, the local community enjoys amenities that were previously unavailable without such robust commercial activity.

Never has the threat to our ecological integrity been more severe than under the current volume of tourism. Notwithstanding fiscal advantages, the natural beauty of our beaches is being compromised by excessive pollution. Unless stricter environmental regulations are implemented, the very charm that attracts visitors will inevitably be destroyed by over-saturation.

Were the municipal authorities to foster a model of sustainable tourism, the current environmental degradation would be mitigated. Striking an equilibrium between development and preservation remains paramount for our future prosperity. Achieving a sustainable future is necessitated by a collective commitment to protecting the heritage that defines our unique identity.

textReconstructor.topic: The Ethical Dilemma of Modern Zoos: Entertainment vs. Animal Welfare

blocks.text: Seldom has the ethicality of maintaining wild creatures in captivity been so fervently contested. While some advocate for zoos as conservation centres, what remains controversial is the inherent conflict between human entertainment and the fundamental rights of sentient beings to exist within their natural habitats.

Furthermore, although it is widely maintained that these institutions facilitate research, substantial evidence suggests that psychological distress is often overlooked. Species are being confined to restrictive enclosures that cannot replicate the complexity of the wild, regardless of the pedagogical benefits claimed by management.

Notwithstanding the revenue generated, the commodification of wildlife for public spectacle is regarded as a moral failure by many. Never should the pursuit of profit be prioritized over the welfare of creatures whose natural behaviours are systematically suppressed for the sake of visitor satisfaction.

Were society to transition toward genuine sanctuaries, we would demonstrate a more mature understanding of ecological stewardship. Governments must prioritize the restoration of natural ecosystems to ensure every species thrives. Ultimately, achieving a harmonious relationship with nature is necessitated by our shared biological future.

pauRules.penaltyRule: REGLA DEL 10%: La coherencia, cohesión y corrección gramatical y ortográfica deben suponer una deducción de al menos el 10% de la nota en cualquier ejercicio de desarrollo. Aplicado por la CRUE en todas las comunidades autónomas desde 2025-2026.
tips: Valencia: En 'For and Against' essays, la introducción debe ser NEUTRAL (sin opinión personal hasta la conclusión). El 40% de la nota de comprensión depende de la corrección expresiva.
Murcia: Prioriza conectores variados y evita repetir estructuras. Usa al menos 3 conectores diferentes por párrafo. Límite: 150-180 palabras.
Andalucía: Se valora especialmente el uso de voz pasiva y construcciones complejas. Las preguntas de Verdadero/Falso requieren justificación textual exacta — sin ella, puntúan 0.
Aragón: El objetivo comunicativo es criterio de evaluación explícito. Usar registro formal en una carta informal puede restar hasta el 25% de la nota de ese bloque. Límite: 120-160 palabras.
General PAU 2026: La optatividad Opción A/B ha desaparecido. El examen es único con optatividad interna limitada. El alumnado debe dominar todo el currículo.
General: NUNCA uses 'I think' o 'In my opinion' en introducción. Reserva opiniones para la conclusión.
Madurez léxica: Sustituye 'palabras baúl' (good, bad, important, thing) por sinónimos B2+/C1 (outstanding, detrimental, paramount, predicament).
Estructura: Intro (hook + thesis) → Body 1 → Body 2 → Conclusion (resumen + opinión final si procede).
Presentación: El orden y la pulcritud son ahora criterios de evaluación explícitos que pueden sumar o restar hasta un 10% de la nota final.
Registro: Usar abreviaturas de chat (u, bc, lol) en una carta formal supone penalización inmediata por falta de adecuación al registro solicitado.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Many teenagers {use} social media {every day}. They can {share} their photos and {discuss about} their problems with friends. However, {actually} this is causing many issues. Some experts {argue} that young people {are very agree} that social media is {harmful}.

errors.explanation: 'Discuss' es transitivo directo en inglés. Nunca 'discuss about' — es calco del español 'discutir sobre'.
Falso amigo clásico PAU. 'Actually' significa 'en realidad', NO 'actualmente'. Usa 'currently' o 'nowadays'.
'Agree' es un verbo, no un adjetivo. Nunca 'be agree'. La forma correcta es simplemente 'agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Climate change {is important} for our future. Governments must {implement} better {policies} to reduce emissions. Scientists {explain me} that the situation is {critical}. {Despite of} these warnings, many countries {continue} burning fossil fuels and {ignoring} the consequences.

errors.explanation: El inglés SIEMPRE requiere sujeto explícito. 'Is important' sin sujeto es calco del español. Debe ser 'It is important'.
'Explain' requiere 'to' antes del pronombre. Nunca 'explain me' — debe ser 'explain to me'.
'Despite' no lleva 'of'. Es 'despite' + sustantivo, o 'in spite of' si quieres usar 'of'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Education {plays} a crucial role in society. Many students {assist to} university every year {hoping} to find a better job. However, {is necessary} to reform the system. Teachers should {make} more creative lessons {so as} students stay motivated. {Furthermore}, investment in education {remains} essential.

errors.explanation: Falso amigo. 'Assist' significa 'ayudar', NO 'asistir a'. Para ir a la universidad usa 'attend university'.
Sujeto omitido — calco del español. En inglés siempre necesitas el 'It': 'It is necessary to...'
'So as' seguido de cláusula completa es incorrecto. Usa 'so that' para introducir propósito con sujeto.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Technology has {changed} our lives dramatically. {Actually}, most people {own} a smartphone. Some {argue} that we {are very depend} on these devices. Others {are agree} that technology brings more benefits. Experts {recommend} {to limit} screen time for children under twelve.

errors.explanation: Falso amigo. 'Actually' = 'en realidad', NO 'actualmente'. Para 'actualmente' usa 'Nowadays' o 'Currently'.
Calco del español 'depender'. En inglés es 'depend on' como verbo. Nunca 'are depend'.
'Agree' es un verbo, no un adjetivo. Nunca 'be agree'. La forma correcta es 'agree'.
Después de 'recommend' se usa el gerundio (-ing). 'Recommend limiting', nunca 'recommend to limit'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Unemployment is a {serious} problem. Young people {are very difficult} to find a job after graduation. Governments must {introduce} new laws to {protect} workers. Many graduates {are agree} that the system is unfair. {Is important} to {invest} in education and training {programmes}.

errors.explanation: Calco del español. El sujeto 'Young people' no puede ser 'difficult'. Correcto: 'find it very difficult to'.
'Agree' es un verbo. Nunca 'be agree'. Correcto: 'agree'.
Sujeto omitido. En inglés siempre 'It is important'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Gender equality {is important} in the workplace. Women {are very difficult} to reach senior positions despite their {qualifications}. Many companies {are agree} that change is needed. Experts {explain me} that unconscious bias {plays} a major role. {Actually}, {significant} progress has been made but much {remains} to be done.

errors.explanation: Sujeto omitido. Correcto: 'It is important' o 'Gender equality is important'.
Calco del español. El sujeto 'Women' no puede ser 'difficult'. Correcto: 'find it very difficult to'.
'Agree' es un verbo. Nunca 'be agree'. Correcto: 'agree'.
Falso amigo. 'Actually' = 'en realidad'. Para 'actualmente' usa 'Currently'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Social inequality {is important} to {address} in modern societies. Governments {are very agree} that more investment is needed. However, {despite of} {strong} economic growth, poverty {persists}. Experts {discuss about} possible solutions {regularly} and {propose} new frameworks.
